note_evidence: long history of colonic polyps requiring repeated removal, progressing over the past year to invasive cancer now involving a "whole stretch of the colon."
rationale: Note documents colon cancer arising from recurrent polyps spanning the colon.

note_evidence: Her specialist advised last month that further disease-directed treatment would cause more harm than benefit; the patient elected to forgo further curative/aggressive treatment and pursue comfort-focused care.
rationale: Specialist advice and patient's election to stop treatment documented.

note_evidence: Remain at home; ... avoid dying in a hospital; and minimize pain.
rationale: Home, comfort, avoid hospital death all documented.

note_evidence: Dull, low abdominal pain, intermittent, most days.
rationale: Matches quality, location, and intermittent timing.

note_evidence: Chronic low back pain for ~20 years (baseline).
Currently controlled to "just about bearable" on medications.
rationale: Pain bearable on current meds documented.

note_evidence: Acetaminophen 325 mg
Simvastatin 10 mg (cholesterol)
Oxycodone extended-release (OxyContin) 10 mg
Oxycodone/acetaminophen 10/325 mg (Percocet) — for "bad stretches"
rationale: Combination drug, dose, and breakthrough indication documented.

note_evidence: Tramadol 50 mg
Naproxen sodium 220 mg
Hydrocodone extended-release 10 mg capsule
Amoxicillin-clavulanate (leftover from dental care)
Fluoride gel
nurse to create written medication schedule clarifying purpose of each medication and identifying breakthrough vs. scheduled agents
rationale: Written schedule with purpose and breakthrough med documented.

note_evidence: Slower than baseline but moving most days
continue fluids and prune juice; report promptly if bowel movements stop
Appetite: Poor; "eats like a bird."
Fatigued; sleeping most afternoons.
History of anemia, which patient feels may contribute.
rationale: Anemia possibly contributing to fatigue documented.

note_evidence: Two-physician hospice certification completed with attending physician.
Formal notifications sent to primary care physician and insurer; hospice now coordinating care.
Physical head-to-toe examination performed by hospice nurse at this visit
Individualized plan of care to be developed documenting patient's goals in her own words: home, comfort, flowers, cat (Biscuit named per patient request).
rationale: Individualized plan of care reflecting wishes documented.

note_evidence: appropriate for and electing home hospice care ... hospice now coordinating care.
Hospital bed — declined at this time; patient prefers own bed.
Grab bars for bathroom — approved by patient.
Skilled nursing visits ~2x/week initially ... Home health aide offered for bathing/dressing. Social worker and chaplain available
24/7 hospice on-call line provided to patient and family.
wants no resuscitation, no machines, no chest compressions ("let me go easy")
Patient clearly declines hospitalization and aggressive interventions.
rationale: Avoidance of hospitalization noted but ambulance transport/ER 'unless requested' not specifically stated.

note_evidence: Next nursing visit in 2 days.
rationale: Nurse follow-up in two days documented.

note_evidence: Niece Karen has been staying overnight most nights and manages medications/paperwork.
Physical head-to-toe examination performed by hospice nurse at this visit
rationale: History and physical exam documented in the encounter note.

note_evidence: At time of visit, pain reported as "about as good as it gets."
rationale: Pain controlled at time of visit documented.
